articles.title: Meet the Eagle Superfans Who Watch Nests 24/7
articles.summary: Imagine a live show starring bald eagles, 75 feet up in a tree! Millions of people watch nest cams to see eaglets hatch and grow. Gloria Gajownik, 71, has watched for 15 years and says the eagles and chat friends feel like family.

articles.title: A Big Boss Battle at the Fed: Will Powell Stay or Go?
articles.summary: The Federal Reserve's leader, Jerome Powell, might not leave when his term ends May 15. He could stay as a regular board member — something no ex-chair has done since 1948. This could cause a "two Popes" problem with the new chair, Kevin Warsh, picked by President Trump.

articles.title: The World Spent $2.88 Trillion on Armies Last Year — That's $350 per Person!
articles.summary: Global military spending hit a record $2.88 trillion in 2025. That's $350 for every person on Earth. The U.S. spent the most — $954 billion, more than the next six countries combined. Since 1949, the U.S. has spent over $53 trillion on its military.
